items.title: Smallcap stocks have already erased Iran war losses. Is a bluechip breakout next?
items.summary: India’s smallcap index has recovered above pre-war levels following the US-Israel-Iran conflict, while the Nifty and midcaps still trade below their late-February peaks, highlighting a sharp market divergence. The smallcap gauge has surged 2.3% to 16,051.40, supported by strong domestic participation. The trend reflects shifting equity ownership patterns, though questions remain on whether the rally can sustain or broaden further.

items.title: AA and BSM ordered to refund learner drivers for hidden fees
items.summary: The owner of the driving schools has been fined for failing to disclose fees upfront online.

items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Benchmark stock indices Sensex and Nifty surged nearly 1 per cent on Wednesday as crude oil prices fell below the $100 per barrel mark amid hopes of renewed diplomatic engagement between the US and Iran.

items.title: Can oil prices above $90-100 over next few months pull Nifty 50 to 20,000 levels? What experts say
items.summary: As the Indian stock markets have moved from earnings-driven to oil-driven trading in the near term, analysts expect higher-for-longer prices to drive another 8-10% correction in Sensex and Nifty 50.

items.title: India aims for cocoa self-sufficiency by 2040 under new roadmap
items.summary: Knowledge paper recommends launch of National Mission on Cocoa, boost R&D and innovation

Top Gainers & Losers on April 15: RailTel Corp, Coforge, Anant Raj, Suzlon Energy, Hindustan Zinc among top gainers
items.summary: On April 13, Indian markets surged as investors reacted positively to potential US-Iran negotiations. The Nifty and BSE Sensex gained 1.63% and 1.64%, respectively, while many stocks in technology and real estate sectors performed well, though a few faced declines.

items.title: Snap's stock jumps 11% on plans to axe 16% of its workforce citing AI efficiencies
items.summary: Snap was up in premarket trading on Wednesday after announcing plans to lay off up to 16% of its global workforce citing AI-driven efficiencies

items.title: ReNew commissions 2.4 GW, marks record renewable capacity addition
items.summary: ReNew Energy Global Plc achieved a record 2.4 GW of new renewable energy capacity in FY2026. This brings their total operating capacity to 12.6 GW, making them the second largest in India. The company also has significant solar manufacturing capabilities. This marks a significant milestone for ReNew's growth in the renewable energy sector.

items.title: Indian rupee ends flat as importer dollar bids wipe boost from oil retreat
items.summary: The Indian rupee closed nearly unchanged ​on Wednesday, wedged between ​comfort from a decline in crude oil prices and ​persistent dollar demand from local importers, including oil marketing companies.

items.summary: United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby has said the next phase for U.S. carriers is figuring out how to better compete on a global stage.

items.title: Echoes of Nuremberg in a new set of corporate trials
items.summary: Jailing of former Lafarge CEO highlights a broader push for corporate accountability

items.title: Power Insider: The markets are obsessed with the Strait of Hormuz. Why it matters less than you think
items.summary: Retail and pro traders are obsessed with gauging the traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, thinking they can gain an edge on where oil prices are going. They can’t.

items.title: Risk-reward has improved; Indian economy structurally more resilient: Rishabh Nahar of Qode Advisors
items.summary: In an interview with Mint, Rishabh Nahar of Qode Advisors discusses the current market structure. With a unique 'all-weather' investment philosophy, he emphasises the importance of long-term capital commitment amidst market fluctuations.

items.title: Rupee settles 9 paise lower at 93.44 against US dollar
items.summary: Strong buying in domestic equities failed to support the rupee even as it gained initially on US-Iran peace talk optimism, say forex analysts

items.title: Iran war 'very close to over,' Trump says — and the stock market 'is going to boom'
items.summary: The U.S. has beaten Iran "militarily" and that the war is "very close to over," Trump said, claiming a peace deal likely.

items.title: European stocks mixed as luxury slump weighs on sentiment
items.summary: European stocks opened in mixed territory on Wednesday as regional traders assess the trajectory of the U.S.-Iran war.

items.title: CG Power arm Axiro, MPower invest in Japanese AI chipmaker EdgeCortix
items.summary: Axiro Semiconductor and MPower Partners have made a significant leap by investing in EdgeCortix, a trailblazing firm from Japan dedicated to advancing energy-efficient AI technology. This funding is set to expedite EdgeCortix’s innovations in next-gen platforms tailored for Generative and Agentic AI applications.

items.title: India gem and jewellery exports rise to ₹2.44 lakh crore in FY26 amid global headwinds
items.summary: In FY26, India's gem and jewellery sector reported an impressive ₹2,44,827.26 crore in exports, demonstrating a modest growth in rupee terms. However, exports in dollar value experienced a downturn amidst global market turbulence. To counter this, exporters expanded their reach, finding new opportunities in the UAE, Australia, and Canada.

items.title: Vikram Solar hits 10 GW milestone in global solar module deployments
items.summary: It is also entering energy storage with plans for a 5 GWh battery facility, aligning with rising clean energy demand.

items.title: Datamatics powers Mumbai Metro Lines 2B & 9 with AFC, mobile ticketing
items.summary: The AFC system enables faster entry and exit at stations, supports automatic fare deduction and is compatible with both QR-coded tickets and common mobility cards

items.title: Amul hits ₹1 lakh crore in sales; MD says India could be dairy to the world within a decade
items.summary: Amul's remarkable growth signals a bright future for India's dairy sector. Recent GST cuts are a game-changer, benefiting farmers and consumers. Value addition and product diversification present significant opportunities. The cooperative model ensures farmers receive a larger share of revenue, positioning India as a global dairy leader. The sector is set for substantial expansion.

items.title: Semiconductor, railway infra stock jumps following Dalal Street rally
items.summary: MIC Electronics shares rose 6% to ₹40.71, driven by strong market interest and recent order wins from Indian Railways. Despite a 48% decline over six months, the stock has surged 4,740% in five years, showcasing significant investor returns.

items.title: Gold prices fall over 10% since US-Iran war. 5 reasons why safe-haven asset is under pressure
items.summary: Gold prices fell on April 15 due to lower demand, dropping 0.23% to ₹1,54,464 per 10 grams. Internationally, spot gold also declined 0.6%. Analysts attribute the drop to rising interest rates, a stronger US dollar, and profit-taking among investors amid geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Why AC maker Blue Star wants both summer and monsoon seasons to be good this year
items.summary: Blue Star’s growth outlook hinges on strong summer-led urban demand and a favourable monsoon supporting rural markets, which now drive over 65% of industry sales. Managing director B Thiagarajan highlighted pricing pressures, geopolitical risks, and raw material inflation as key challenges, while noting stable demand and inventory levels. The festival season outlook remains uncertain amid global and domestic headwinds.

items.title: Mahindra eyes South Africa expansion as China rivals gain ground
items.summary: Mahindra & Mahindra is reportedly in advanced talks to upgrade its South African plant, potentially introducing completely knocked-down (CKD) production. This move aims to capitalize on rising demand for affordable vehicles and sidestep potential import tariffs, enhancing local manufacturing capabilities and competition in the South African market.

items.title: JioHotstar brings HBO Max to India in exclusive deal
items.summary: HBO Max is now available in India exclusively on JioHotstar. This partnership brings popular shows like Euphoria and The Last of Us to Indian viewers. Upcoming releases include new seasons of House of the Dragon and the Harry Potter series. Subscribers can also access classic series and Warner Bros. films.

items.title: Penny stock hits 5% upper circuit amid bull trend on Dalal Street
items.summary: Penny stock AVI Polymers today announced the launch of the smart farming application, KrishiBuddy, by its subsidiary, as it looks to create highly scalable and technology-driven revenue streams.

items.title: Iranian oil arrives in India just ahead of US waiver expiry
items.summary: India has received Iranian oil shipments. This marks the first imports in seven years. The nation is securing volumes before a US grace period ends. Tankers are discharging crude on India's east and west coasts. This move aims to manage global energy flow disruptions. India is leveraging waivers for previously restricted oil.

items.title: Gold surges past $4,840; silver tops $80 as dollar weakens and Iran tensions ease
items.summary: Precious metals post sharp gains on hopes of fresh US-Iran dialogue; crude oil falls sharply while rupee stays under pressure.

items.title: Banks’ soaring exposure to trading firms creating ‘inherent fragility’, warns S&P
items.summary: Large investment banks have become increasingly reliant on markets financing, report says

items.title: India’s March trade deficit narrows to $20.67 billion on year
items.summary: India’s March trade deficit narrows on year as imports ease, but elevated energy, gold, and electronics purchases and high freight costs could keep the gap wide.

items.title: Suzlon Energy shares surge 8%, extend 5-day rally as institutional investors raise stake in Q4
items.summary: Suzlon Energy shares surged 7.6% on April 14, marking five consecutive days of gains and a cumulative 19% increase. Institutional investors raised their stake to 4.87%, despite previous pressure.

items.title: Gold rises 60% since last Akshaya Tritiya. Should you invest this year amid Iran war uncertainty?
items.summary: Gold prices have surged nearly 60% since Akshaya Tritiya 2025, but the outlook for further gains by 2027 is complex due to geopolitical tensions and interest rate uncertainty. Despite near-term headwinds, structural factors like central bank buying and rising debt support a positive long-term view, suggesting gold as a hedge.

items.title: Russia offers China energy lifeline as the Iran war strangles global supply
items.summary: "Russia can certainly fill the resource gap that has arisen in China," Russian foreign minister Sergei Lavrov said Wednesday.

items.title: Nifty rebounds 8% in April after March rout, but stays below pre-war levels. What does it signal?
items.summary: Nifty has rebounded 8% in April after a sharp March correction but remains below pre-war levels, signalling cautious recovery. The bounce is driven by short covering and selective buying, while persistent FII outflows, elevated crude prices and geopolitical risks continue to weigh on sentiment despite improving valuations.

items.title: Stock market today: Nifty 50 reclaims 24,000. What are the next crucial technical levels for the index?
items.summary: Stock market today: Analysts cautioned on the uncertainty around foreign institutional investor (FII) outflows, and potential earnings downgrades, which could limit valuation expansion.

items.title: India unveils a power-packed FTA calendar: UK, Oman, New Zealand deals next
items.summary: In 2026, India will embark on an ambitious journey to amplify its role in global commerce. The commencement of pivotal free trade agreements with the United Kingdom and Oman, alongside an impending partnership with New Zealand, highlights India's strategic focus on fostering robust international connections. This proactive approach demonstrates the nation's dedication to turning diplomatic negotiations into thriving economic collaborations.

items.title: Mukul Agrawal buys 25 lakh shares in soaring smallcap bank as other lenders falter
items.summary: Ace investor Mukul Mahavir Agrawal increased his stake in Jammu & Kashmir Bank during the January–March quarter, adding around 25 lakh shares. The lender has seen strong stock gains this year alongside steady operational improvement, supported by rising profitability, improving asset quality and sustained growth in advances and deposits.

items.title: 12 stocks held by 100+ MFs surge up to 55% in just over 3 months
items.summary: As of March 2026, data shows that 259 stocks were held by more than 100 mutual fund schemes, highlighting broad-based institutional interest. ETMarkets’ analysis reveals that 50 of these stocks have delivered gains of more than 10% since the start of CY26.

items.title: LIC share price gains 5% after board approves 1:1 bonus issue; up over 15% in April
items.summary: LIC bonus issue: LIC shares surged 5% to ₹841.30 after a 1:1 bonus issue approval, marking three consecutive days of gains. The company proposes to issue 632.49 crore equity shares, increasing its capital to ₹12,649.99 crore.

items.title: Share buybacks this month: Wipro, Aurobindo Pharma, 2 other stocks to watch out for
items.summary: Four companies—Wipro, Cyient, Windlas Biotech, and Aurobindo Pharma—are set to announce or act on share buybacks this month, with Aurobindo’s record date due soon. Buybacks involve companies repurchasing shares to boost value, use surplus cash, or strengthen ownership. Meanwhile, new rules under the Finance Bill 2026 impose a 12% surcharge on buyback-related capital gains for promoters, while other investors remain taxed as per existing norms.

items.title: Saurabh Mukherjea has moved half his personal portfolio out of India; here's why
items.summary: Saurabh Mukherjea, founder of Marcellus Investment Managers, has shifted his personal portfolio to a 50-50 split between Indian and global investments, reflecting a strategic pivot. The firm is reducing domestic consumption exposure and increasing bets on export-oriented businesses, while also building a position in companies set to benefit from rising NPAs in the Indian banking system.

items.title: India’s trade deficit narrows to $20.67 billion in March on West Asia risks
items.summary: India's trade deficit saw a significant reduction in March. Exports increased while imports decreased. This improvement occurred despite rising tensions in West Asia. Geopolitical risks could impact future trade. Services exports continue to drive growth for India. Merchandise exports also saw a modest rise.

items.title: In Charts: India slips to 6th in GDP ranking for now; to reach 4th by FY28
items.summary: A downward revision in GDP estimates and currency effects push India below the UK. The climb to the fourth place is now deferred to FY28.

items.title: NBFCs' reliance on bank borrowings to increase in FY27 on lower interest rates
items.summary: In the next fiscal year, non-banking finance companies are likely to increase their borrowing from banks due to the attractive lower interest rates available. Additionally, the unpredictable geopolitical landscape is making external commercial borrowings less appealing. To mitigate funding challenges, NBFCs may turn to securitisation for support.

items.title: India's 30-year economic model is over; time to pivot from IT to manufacturing: Saurabh Mukherjea
items.summary: Saurabh Mukherjea believes India's long-standing economic model is concluding. He anticipates rising inflation and a weakening rupee impacting consumer spending. Interest rate hikes are also expected, posing challenges for lenders. The focus is shifting towards manufacturing exports, benefiting from global trends. This transition presents new investment opportunities in specific export-oriented sectors.

items.title: Muthoot Finance seeks shareholder nod for entry into insurance distribution biz
items.summary: Muthoot Finance is gearing up for a pivotal growth phase as it seeks shareholder approval to venture into the insurance distribution arena. This bold step encompasses a wide range of offerings, including life, general, and health insurance products, marking the company's ambition to become a comprehensive corporate agent in India's insurance landscape.

items.title: Massive boost for PSU banks! Supreme Court closes Sterling Biotech case with ₹9,800 crore recovery
items.summary: Over the course of the proceedings, the Sandesara group reportedly repaid ₹3,507 crore directly to the lending consortium, while an additional ₹1,192 crore was realised through liquidation processes.

items.title: Trump renews criticism of UK, saying it should ‘drill, baby, drill’ for North Sea oil
items.summary: Trump's comments come at a time when the U.K. government has said the Iran war energy shock shows the need “to get off the fossil fuel rollercoaster.”

items.title: Beyond metros, India bets big on specialized investment funds
items.summary: High-ticket funds pitched as “conservative” are drawing investors from smaller towns, led by hybrid strategies and distributor push.

items.title: Wipro to buy select contracts from California IT firm Alpha Net for $71 million to enhance AI, consulting services
items.summary: The deals aim to enhance Wipro's AI-driven services and are expected to boost annual revenue by $173.3 million amid macroeconomic uncertainties.

items.title: 3,700% return in five years! Small-cap stock hits upper circuit following positive sentiments on Dalal Street
items.summary: The small-cap stock witnessed an upward trend after the Indian stock market opened with strong gains on Wednesday. Here's its recent busines updates, along with share price trend.

items.title: U.S. Treasury yields hold steady as markets eye Middle East resolution
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields were unchanged Wednesday as investors weighed the latest developments in the Middle East.

items.title: Mideast Conflict Could Still Trigger Market Turmoil, IMF Warns
items.summary: Conflict in the Middle East could yet trigger the kind of turmoil that markets have so far avoided, the International Monetary Fund warned, adding its voice to a rising chorus of concern about the financial stability impact of the war.

items.title: Indian agricultural sector under threat by trifecta of below-Normal Monsoon, El Nino, and West Asia war: ICRA
items.summary: India's agriculture sector may face a challenging year ahead amid concerns over weak monsoon, possible El Nino conditions, and fertiliser supply risks linked to the West Asia conflict, according to a report by ICRA.

items.title: Thames Water should go into administration, investor says
items.summary: The UK is “sleepwalking” into bad deal on Thames Water says a frustrated rival bidder.

items.title: 20 years on, 3,000 Agra homebuyers seek UP govt intervention over stalled Ansal township
items.summary: Over 3,000 homebuyers of Ansal Properties' Sushant Taj City in Agra are seeking possession of plots booked nearly two decades ago. Despite paying in full, buyers are frustrated by the lack of progress and clarity from the Uttar Pradesh government and the Agra Development Authority, which has a stake in the project.

items.title: Can crude oil volatility, El Niño fears derail Indian economy's growth momentum? What should investors do?
items.summary: As crude oil prices swing and El Niño threatens monsoon forecasts, experts weigh the potential impact on India's economy. Experts say Idia's macro construct remains strong but will become vulnerable.

items.title: Propshare Celestia SM REIT IPO Day 3: Issue booked 86% so far. Check GMP, issue details, key dates
items.summary: The Propshare Celestia SM REIT IPO runs from April 10 to April 16, offering units priced between ₹10-10.50 lakh. Institutional investors acquired 17% of units, with an 86% subscription status reported by day 3.

items.title: Gold loans soar in India, but it’s southern households leading the charge
items.summary: Gold loans in India have surged fourfold since March 2022, becoming the second-largest retail credit segment. Southern states, led by Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Karnataka, continue to dominate originations, accounting for over half the market. While the south remains the core, emerging regions like Uttar Pradesh are showing significant growth from a lower base.

items.title: CleanMax, Sangam India partner for hybrid renewable energy supply in Rajasthan
items.summary: CleanMax Enviro Energy Solutions and Sangam India have joined forces. This partnership aims to reduce carbon emissions for Sangam India's operations in Rajasthan. A hybrid renewable energy supply agreement has been signed. It will provide solar and wind power, supported by battery storage. This will power five Sangam facilities across Rajasthan.

items.title: ICICI Pru Life: Moving in right direction, but still doesn’t justify rosy outlook embedded in valuation
items.summary: ICICI Pru Life’s Q4 shows strong protection growth and margin gains, but FY26 growth stayed muted and FY27 outlook remains modest. Does a 28–29x VNB valuation leave room for error?

items.title: The Iranians have a vote: Analyst warns markets are ignoring fragile ceasefire as talks loom
items.summary: Markets may be rallying on hopes of peace, but Viking Research’s John Rossomando warns that investors are underestimating geopolitical risks. He cautions that any aggressive move by Iran, such as renewed missile strikes or attacks on shipping, could quickly reverse market optimism and disrupt global trade.

items.title: U.S. crude trades lower as possible Washington-Tehran talks raise hopes for Mideast peace deal
items.summary: Oil prices traded mixed as optimism grew that Iran may restart negotiations with the U.S. and Israel, easing tensions over the conflict that has disrupted the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Wholesale inflation rises to a 3-year high of 3.88% in March
items.summary: India’s wholesale inflation rose to a 38-month high of about 3.9% in March from 2.1% in February, driven by higher prices of crude oil, power and manufactured goods amid the West Asia conflict.

items.title: Banks to report steady profit rise as loans grow, treasury drags
items.summary: Indian banks are expected to ​report a steady profit rise ​in the January-March quarter, four brokers said, aided by credit ​growth and liquidity buffers, while higher bond yields and forex arbitrage curbs weighed on treasury income.

items.title: HDFC Bank, SBI to Urban Company: Here's what mutual funds bought and sold the most amid stock market crash in March
items.summary: The equity funds recorded strong net inflows of ₹40,500 crore last month, up sharply from ₹26,000 crore in February, a 56% increase, even as the Indian stock market's benchmark indices posted their worst monthly fall in six years amid the US-Iran war-led selloff.

items.title: Sebi makes it easier for firms to cut IPO size after Iran war hits sentiment, email shows
items.summary: Securities and Exchange Board of India has eased norms allowing firms to reduce IPO sizes amid weak market sentiment triggered by the Iran conflict, giving issuers flexibility to navigate volatility and investor caution.

items.title: Pakistan’s KSE-100 surges 10,000 points in 2 days on US-Iran peace talk optimism
items.summary: Pakistan's stock markets surged for a second consecutive session, with the KSE-100 index gaining significantly amid hopes of renewed US-Iran peace talks. Hopes for a resolution to the conflict, potentially restoring oil flows, have boosted investor confidence. This follows a period of high volatility, with the market showing sharp returns during phases of macro stabilization linked to IMF support.

items.title: VinFast expands India lineup with VF MPV 7: Price, specs, availability
items.summary: VinFast has launched its new seven-seater electric MPV, the VF MPV 7, in India. This marks the company's entry into a new segment. The vehicle offers a range of 517 km. VinFast is expanding its retail presence and aims for 75 showrooms by 2026. The VF MPV 7 is locally assembled in Tamil Nadu.

items.title: Chinese robotaxi companies forge ahead with UAE expansion despite Iran war
items.summary: Ride-hailing company Didi plans to begin its first overseas robotaxi test in the United Arab Emirates later this year.

items.title: Wipro Q4 Preview: Profit may fall on margin pressures. Is buyback the only thing to cheer?
